title: parasenecio katoanus
intro: Parasenecio katoanus, also known as thread-leaf ragwort, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to have medicinal benefits in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Parasenecio katoanus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. Additionally, its antiseptic properties make it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for wound healing and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
